goal: goal: build a generic hosted ai platform

events.content: goal: ship a self-hosted long-term memory runtime with low drift

constraint: self-hosted first
constraint: keep api stable
constraint: make memory decisions auditable

Architecture update:
Project Memory now treats the assistant runtime as a first-class product boundary. Runtime sessions should preserve evidence-backed answers, explicit write tiers, and digest trigger policy notes. This long-form spec is intentionally document-heavy so memory profiles can choose whether to preserve it as documented memory or reduce it to candidate context.
We decide to expose assistant runtime policy profiles

Evaluation protocol update:
Replay consistency, contradiction tracking, and runtime evidence coverage should be reported together. The benchmark should also surface which runtime policy profile was used, so developers can compare conservative and document-heavy behavior under the same fixture.
We decide to measure replay consistency separately from throughput
Status update: long-form notes are now common in runtime integrations
Noise: quick aside about optional UI demos later
